title: Crassocephalum bojeri
intro: Crassocephalum bojeri, also known as Bojer's crassocephalum or Madagascar crassocephalum, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in tropical Africa and Madagascar. The leaves of Crassocephalum bojeri have been used in traditional medicine for their health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Crassocephalum bojeri is often prepared as a tea or decoction, and its leaves are also used in infusions and tinctures to harness their medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Crassocephalum bojeri is classified within the family Asteraceae, which comprises flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. The genus Crassocephalum is a group of African plants that are native to tropical regions. Crassocephalum bojeri is a species within the Crassocephalum genus. It is further classified as a member of the tribe Senecioneae, which consists of daisy-like plants. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.
